summary: Full MFA (TOTP) + DPP/Compliance test for Amazon DPP uyumu. Backend: 12/12 pytest cases passed in /app/backend/tests/test_mfa_compliance.py. Frontend: end-to-end MFA enable -> login challenge -> verify -> disable + Compliance page (MfaCard, PII retention, 14-item DPP checklist) all working. CRITICAL: MFA was successfully DISABLED at the end of testing — confirmed via plain login (admin@facette.com/admin123) returning {token, user.is_admin:true} with no mfa_required. Next testing agent can use admin123 plain login without MFA.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/routes/mfa.py:139 — create_token(user['id'], user.get('is_admin', False)) is correct; good that MFA secret is encrypted via security.crypto vault and not stored plain. Recommend adding short-lived rate-limit on /auth/mfa/verify to prevent online brute force of 6-digit codes (currently no limiter decorator; with valid_window=1 there are ~3 acceptable codes out of 10^6, so a fast attacker can succeed in O(300K) requests — add @limiter.limit('10/minute') and/or per-mfa_token attempt counter).

backend/routes/mfa.py:101-115 — /auth/mfa/disable accepts existing Bearer token but the token itself was originally minted BEFORE MFA enable. This is fine for normal flow but means an attacker who stole an old JWT can disable MFA without a fresh login. Mitigation: re-issue JWT on enable (invalidate old) OR require recent re-auth (jti+iat check).
backend/routes/auth.py:128 — plain bcrypt+rate-limit before MFA gate is correct. Good: failed_login counters reset BEFORE returning mfa_required.

frontend/src/pages/admin/Compliance.jsx:171 — MFA disable uses window.prompt() for code entry — works but is a UX regression vs. the inline 6-digit input used for enable. Consider replacing with the same styled input used for enable.
frontend/src/pages/admin/Compliance.jsx:196 — Manual secret displayed in plain text (good for transcription) but should warn user 'do not share' and ideally be click-to-copy.
frontend/src/pages/admin/AdminLogin.jsx:111 — MFA form shown but no resend / re-init link if mfa_token expires (5min TTL); user must hit Back -> re-enter password. Acceptable for admin UX.
test_credentials: admin@facette.com / admin123 — MFA currently DISABLED (confirmed). pyotp.TOTP(secret).now() used for TOTP codes during the test.
seed_data_creation: None. Test temporarily flipped pii_retention.days (25 -> restored to original) and toggled mfa_enabled on admin user (enabled then disabled at cleanup). Final admin user state: mfa_enabled=false, mfa_secret_enc unset.
context_for_next_testing_agent: MFA is disabled. Plain login (admin@facette.com/admin123) works without MFA challenge. /admin/dpp-uyum renders with MfaCard, PII retention (PII save toast = 'Ayar kaydedildi'), and 14-item DPP checklist. If you need to retest MFA, expect that enabling it requires both DB write + token refresh; remember to always disable via POST /api/auth/mfa/disable with a fresh TOTP code OR via direct mongo update before finishing.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no defects found this iteration.
